qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Share token bypasses basic auth on WebDAV endpoint granting full file system access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes a share token bypassing authentication on the WebDAV endpoint due to improper scope restriction in BasicAuthMiddleware (httpserver/middleware.go:22-31). This is a different root cause than the advisory-described vulnerability, which is about missing .goshs file-based ACL enforcement on state-changing routes (PUT, POST /upload, ?mkdir, ?delete) in httpserver/updown.go and httpserver/handler.go. The advisory vulnerability allows unauthenticated write operations in .goshs-protected directories without any token, while the finding requires obtaining a valid share token first.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes missing .goshs file-based ACL enforcement on state-changing routes (PUT, POST /upload, ?mkdir, ?delete) in goshs, allowing unauthenticated file operations in protected directories. The share_token_webdav_auth_bypass finding describes a different authentication bypass mechanism where share tokens lack scope restrictions on WebDAV.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-wvhv-qcqf-f3cx describes missing .goshs ACL checks on state-changing routes in httpserver/updown.go (PUT, upload) and httpserver/handler.go (?mkdir, ?delete)
The share_token_webdav_auth_bypass finding root cause is in httpserver/middleware.go:22-31 (share token bypass without scope restriction)
The share token bypass requires obtaining a valid share token first, while the advisory vulnerability requires no token at all
